full_prompt: Design a single-part monolithic fin-can (three-fin integrated to a cylindrical body section) for a university sounding rocket with 15,000 ft apogee, thrust-to-weight ratio > 5 at ignition, delivering a >= 2 kg payload. The fin-can shall be a single machined / 3D-printed monolithic part with three clipped-delta fins distributed at 120 deg around a cylindrical aft body section (outer diameter 155 mm, wall thickness to be sized). Fin planform: root chord 200 mm, tip chord 80 mm, span (root to tip) 110 mm, sweep angle (LE) 35 deg, airfoil NACA 0006. Material: 6061-T6 aluminum, E = 68.9 GPa, yield = 276 MPa, density = 2700 kg/m^3. Expected maximum dynamic pressure q_max = 90,000 Pa at Mach 1.4 during ascent. Load cases: (R1) aerodynamic fin bending moment from a peak angle-of-attack of 5 deg at q_max yielding a normal force of 800 N per fin applied at 40% chord, producing a root bending moment of 35 N*m; max von Mises in fin root fillet <= yield / 1.5 = 184 MPa. (R2) thrust transfer: motor thrust of 4,000 N peak is transferred into the fin-can via the aft motor mount retainer ring (8x M5 bolts on 140 mm PCD); local bearing stress around bolt holes <= yield / 1.5 = 184 MPa. (R3) aeroelastic flutter margin: fin first torsional divergence speed (computed by modal + aerodynamic coupling, or Theodorsen approximation) shall be >= 1.2 x Mach 1.4 at sea-level equivalent airspeed; modal analysis shall return fin first-torsion frequency >= 200 Hz when the fin-can body is hard-mounted. (R4) recovery deceleration shock: during main-parachute opening (main_mass = 12 kg, opening shock 8 g) an axial tensile load of 12 kg * 8 g = 941 N is transmitted through the forward coupler interface; max von Mises <= 184 MPa. (R5) mass of fin-can <= 1.2 kg. Primary verification structural FEA (linear static for R1/R2/R4) + modal analysis (R3). Thermal, fluid, and radiation analyses explicitly NOT required for this gate (aerothermal heating on Mach 1.4 aluminum fins is below 80 C and treated as out of scope). Deliverables: STEP AP242, a report with fin root fillet stress (MPa), first torsional modal frequency (Hz), mass (kg).
short_prompt:
Design a single-part monolithic three-fin aluminum fin-can for a 15,000 ft-apogee university sounding rocket (T/W > 5 at ignition, >= 2 kg payload). The fin-can is a cylindrical aft body section with three clipped-delta fins at 120 deg; root chord 200 mm, tip chord 80 mm, span 110 mm, leading-edge sweep 35 deg, NACA 0006 profile. An 8x M5 aft motor-mount retainer ring sits on a 140 mm PCD.

Envelope: body section OD 155 mm. Expected flight peak q_max = 90 kPa at Mach 1.4.

Load cases.
- LC1 aerodynamic fin bending: 800 N per fin at 40% chord (peak 5 deg AoA at q_max), 35 N*m fin-root moment.
- LC2 thrust transfer: 4,000 N peak motor thrust reacted through the 8-bolt motor-mount ring, local bearing check.
- LC3 flutter / modal: fin first torsional divergence speed >= 1.2 x Mach 1.4 (sea-level equivalent airspeed); hard-mounted fin first-torsion modal floor.
- LC4 recovery shock: 941 N axial tension through the forward coupler (main parachute, 12 kg, 8 g opening).

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: R3 is formally a flutter/divergence analysis (Theodorsen or panel-method coupled to modal). FEA modal frequency is the conservative proxy.

prompt.title: Three-fin monolithic sounding rocket fin-can (15,000 ft)
prompt.brief: 6061-T6 monolithic fin-can for 15 kft university sounding rocket. Sized for q_max 90 kPa at Mach 1.4 and 4 kN thrust through 8-bolt retainer.

load_cases.name: modal
load_cases.description: hard-mounted body; extract fin torsion mode

boundary_conditions.mounting: forward coupler interface fixed for structural, full body fixed for modal
boundary_conditions.fin_loads: distributed pressure statically equivalent to 800 N normal force

exclusions_explained.thermal_analysis: Aerothermal <80 C on Mach 1.4 aluminum, out of scope here.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Aero reduced to equivalent static pressure from trajectory analysis.
exclusions_explained.buckling_analysis: Fin is bending dominated, not a buckling driver.

eval_coverage: aerodynamic bending + thrust interface + shock combined
